Title: Jasmonate response decay and defense metabolite accumulation contributes to age-regulated dynamics of plant insect resistance

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ repressor protein with N-terminal ZIM domain and C-terminal Jas domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High JAZ3 protein accumulation attenuates jasmonate response and reduces insect resistance; it indirectly opposes defense metabolite-associated resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S:JAZ3-HA overexpression lines with different protein levels; immunoblotting across ages; yeast two-hybrid; pull-down; yeast three-hybrid; MeJA response assays; insect feeding assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript did not change significantly among first three leaf pairs, but protein accumulated with plant age
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein abundance increased in older leaves; reduced in 35S:MIR156 and elevated in SPL9:rSPL9 background
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; plant age; wounding; insect feeding; long-day and short-day growth
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated JAZ3 weakens JA-inducible defense and increases susceptibility to herbivores
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ3 protein accumulates with plant age without corresponding transcriptional increase. 2. SPL9 promotes JAZ3 accumulation by interacting with its N-terminus and weakening COI1-JAZ3 interaction. 3. High JAZ3 levels attenuate JA-responsive gene induction and reduce resistance to Helicoverpa armigera.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Age sensitivity depends on intact N- and C-terminal regions; JAZ3dN and JAZ3dC lose age-dependent accumulation behavior. The paper focuses on JAZ3 as a signaling repressor affecting defense, not as a biosynthetic enzyme for glucosinolate production.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against insect herbivores and pathogens
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by LC-MS and UPLC-MS; no public accession reported

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box jasmonate receptor component of SCF ubiquitin E3 ligase
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JA-insensitive coi1-2 plants have reduced glucosinolate levels and reduced insect resistance, but age-dependent glucosinolate accumulation still occurs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coi1-2 mutant analysis; insect feeding assays; LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; JA-response gene assays
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: JAZ3; SPL9 (no obvious direct interaction detected)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein level did not noticeably change with age in tested leaves
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Impaired JA signaling decreases inducible defense
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COI1 is required for normal JA-dependent insect defense. 2. Age-related increase of glucosinolate accumulation is not abolished in coi1-2. 3. COI1 protein level does not explain age-dependent JAZ3 accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because mutant background was central for testing causal relation between JA signaling and defense metabolite accumulation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against insect herbivores and pathogens
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; UPLC-MS hormone profiling; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme for indole-related glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an early committed conversion in indole glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for indole glucosinolate production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for accumulation of indole glucosinolates, but loss of CYP79B2 together with CYP79B3 has only mild effect on H. armigera growth because indole glucosinolates are a minor proportion of total leaf glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cyp79b2 cyp79b3 double mutant; LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; insect feeding assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces indole glucosinolates but may have limited effect on total herbivore resistance in this context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B2 functions in indole glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. cyp79b2 cyp79b3 greatly reduces indole glucosinolates. 3. Reduced indole glucosinolates cause only mild increase in bollworm growth in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as pathway evidence rather than the main mechanistic focus of the paper.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS and GC-MS metabolite profiling; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme for indole-related glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an early committed conversion in indole glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for indole glucosinolate production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with CYP79B2 to support indole glucosinolate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cyp79b2 cyp79b3 double mutant; LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; insect feeding assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B3 is a key indole glucosinolate biosynthetic enzyme. 2. Combined loss with CYP79B2 greatly decreases indole glucosinolates. 3. The effect on generalist larval growth is mild in this leaf context.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paired with CYP79B2 in mutant analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS and GC-MS metabolite profiling; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for aliphatic glucosinolate production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript abundance was lower in older plants despite higher glucosinolate content, arguing that age-related glucosinolate accumulation is unlikely due to increased de novo biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR across ages; LC-MS glucosinolate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lower in 40-day-old than 20-day-old plants; increasing during progression of first leaves toward senescence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79F1 expression does not parallel glucosinolate accumulation with age. 2. Higher glucosinolate storage in older leaves is unlikely explained by enhanced CYP79F1 transcription. 3. Supports remobilization/storage model rather than biosynthetic upregulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as marker of pathway transcription rather than direct causal manipulation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; qRT-PCR; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for aliphatic glucosinolate production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression declines in old plants despite higher glucosinolate accumulation, supporting non-biosynthetic explanation for age-dependent metabolite increase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR across ages; LC-MS glucosinolate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lower in 40-day-old than 20-day-old plants; increased in aging first leaves while glucosinolates decline
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79F2 transcript pattern is opposite to glucosinolate storage dynamics with age. 2. Supports metabolite remobilization/storage model. 3. Not shown as a decisive control point in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker gene for biosynthetic transcription status.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; qRT-PCR; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression decreased in older plants despite higher glucosinolate content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR across ages; LC-MS glucosinolate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP83A1 transcript abundance does not explain age-dependent glucosinolate accumulation. 2. Supports post-biosynthetic storage/remobilization contribution. 3. Used as pathway expression marker.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; qRT-PCR; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression decreased in older plants while glucosinolate storage increased
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR across ages; LC-MS glucosinolate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP83B1 expression is inversely related to age-related glucosinolate accumulation. 2. This argues against de novo synthesis as main cause of increased mature-leaf glucosinolates. 3. Supports remobilization/storage hypothesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; qRT-PCR; no accession reported

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE transcription factor with SBP domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Jasmonate-responsive defense signaling that influences defense-metabolite-associated insect resistance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein stabilization of JAZ repressors via direct interaction, thereby attenuating JA-responsive transcription
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Age-dependent feedforward-like attenuation driven by increasing SPL9 as miR156 declines
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JAZ3 and other JAZ proteins (JAZ1; JAZ3; JAZ4; JAZ6; JAZ10; JAZ11)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes JAZ3 accumulation and thereby represses induction of JA-responsive genes LOX2; VSP2; TAT1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SPL9 negatively regulates JA response and insect resistance by stabilizing JAZ repressors; it does not explain age-related glucosinolate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: miR156 overexpression and mimic lines; miR156-resistant SPL9 overexpression; inducible SPL9:rSPL9-GR with DEX; qRT-PCR; insect feeding assays; yeast two-hybrid; pull-down; immunoblotting; yeast three-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: JAZ1; JAZ3; JAZ4; JAZ6; JAZ10; JAZ11
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts through interaction with JAZ proteins; weakens COI1-JAZ3 association
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; cytoplasm-to-nucleus translocation in GR inducible system after DEX
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein level gradually increases with plant age in sequential new leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in SPL9:rSPL9 and low in 35S:MIR156; age-associated increase
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SPL9 native promoter in SPL9:rSPL9-GR and GFP-SPL9 constructs; 35S via miR156 manipulation background
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plant age; DEX induction; MeJA; insect feeding; long-day and short-day growth
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher SPL9 dampens defense and increases insect susceptibility, but may help balance growth-defense costs during maturation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of age-related decay of JA-mediated defense, but not the main driver of glucosinolate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPL9 accumulation increases with plant age and directly attenuates JA responsiveness. 2. SPL9 binds several JAZ proteins, especially JAZ3, and promotes JAZ3 stabilization by interfering with COI1-mediated degradation. 3. SPL9 reduces insect resistance, and this effect is abolished in the coi1-2 background, linking SPL9 action to JA signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SPL9 is a developmental regulator, the paper shows its defense role mainly through protein interaction rather than transcriptional activation. It is miR156-targeted.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against insect herbivores and pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS and UPLC-MS metabolite profiling; no public accession reported

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal aliphatic glucosinolate accumulation and contributes strongly to insect resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: myb28 myb29 double mutant; LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; insect feeding assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functional overlap with MYB29 inferred from double mutant
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Greatly reduced glucosinolates cause increased herbivore growth
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator of aliphatic glucosinolate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB28 is required with MYB29 for normal aliphatic glucosinolate accumulation. 2. myb28 myb29 leaves are highly susceptible to H. armigera. 3. Residual glucosinolates still increase with age in the double mutant, so age-dependent accumulation is not solely governed by MYB28/MYB29.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because mutant analysis directly links regulator-dependent glucosinolate levels to insect resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against insect herbivores and pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; no accession reported

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with MYB28 to maintain aliphatic glucosinolate levels and insect resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: myb28 myb29 double mutant; LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; insect feeding assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functional overlap with MYB28 inferred from double mutant
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator of aliphatic glucosinolate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB29 functions with MYB28 as a major positive regulator of aliphatic glucosinolates. 2. Loss in the myb28 myb29 mutant strongly decreases glucosinolate levels and resistance. 3. Age-related increase of residual glucosinolates persists, indicating additional mechanisms beyond MYB29-dependent biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against insect herbivores and pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS glucosinolate profiling; no accession reported
